LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT KAMALA HARRIS' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PENAL CODE 11126(C) PROVIDES AN ADMINISTRATIVE-ADJUDICATION REMEDY IF A GOVERNMENT ENTITY "DENIES THE ALLEGATIONS OF INACCURACY OR INCOMPLETENESS." PETITIONER DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT SHE EXHAUSTED THIS REMEDY. I THUS LACK JURISDICTION OVER THE MATTER AND THE DEMURRER MUST BE SUSTAINED. PETITIONER ALSO FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT EXHAUSTING HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDY WOULD BE FUTILE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332, BUS. TEL: 415-551-3745. (D302)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, James Farragher Campbell, Esq. (415-766-3542) appears as counsel for petitioner

Deputy Attorney General, Courtney S. Lui of California Justice Department (510-622-2115) appears as counsel for respondent

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER BONNIE KATZ'S petition for writ of mandate is OFF CALENDAR as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, James Farragher Campbell, Esq. (415-766-3542) appears as counsel for petitioner/opposing party

Deputy Attorney General, Courtney S. Lui of California Justice Department (510-622-2115) appears as counsel for respondent/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT KAMALA HARRIS' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Penal Code 11126(c) provides an administrative-adjudication remedy if a government entity "denies the allegations of inaccuracy or incompleteness." Petitioner does not allege that she exhausted this remedy. I thus lack jurisdiction over the matter and the demurrer must be sustained. Petitioner also failed to demonstrate that exhausting her administrative remedy would be futile. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-10-2016 Continued To Aug-02-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

PETITIONER BONNIE KATZ Notice Of Hearing is continued to August 2, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 per the agreement of the parties.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/SB)
